name: Fairy Fusion Internal Review Adapter

source.notes: Internal bounded multi-reviewer orchestration. OpenRouter Fusion, Subagent, Advisor, and agent-system design-space papers are used only as public design references; this adapter does not call OpenRouter.

capabilities: bounded automatic trigger decisions for plugin-managed harnesses
append-only sidechain review artifacts with compact synthesis return

inputs: automatic trigger state when decision mode is used
outputs: decision-only trigger record when automatic check mode is used
evidence: source docs checked date for design references
executor model and judge model when executed
trigger reasons, reviewer cap, recursion depth and cap, artifact path, and input SHA-256

boundaries: Use Fairy Fusion only when the cost of being wrong exceeds the cost of extra reviewer passes.
Cap panel size and recursion depth before launching reviewers.
Keep legal reviewers evidence-grounded and do not present outputs as legal advice.
Do not send confidential matter files to external providers without explicit authorization.
Label this as Fairy Fusion or fusion-style review, not OpenRouter Fusion.
Keep each reviewer isolated: task context and output schema only, no parent conversation transcript.
For automatic activation, trigger on repeated failure signatures, empty or meaningless artifacts, missing validation ledgers, review conflicts, or explicit requests rather than benchmark sample IDs.
Automatic checks decide only; they do not launch reviewers, call providers, retry work, or exceed recursion depth 1.
forbidden_claims: A majority vote is sufficient for legal completeness.
Fairy Fusion is the OpenRouter Fusion API.
Fairy Fusion uses OpenRouter unless an integration explicitly says so.
Advisor or subagent output can override benchmark evidence without validation.

checks: source documentation checked date is recorded
automatic_execution is false and recursion_cap is 1
synthesis includes contradictions and blind spots
minimum_artifacts: execution mode: reviewer outputs and synthesis table
automatic decision mode: validated state and trigger decision
retry result summary when a retrying integration is explicitly enabled
